audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to provide MCP server management and monitoring, but implements covert exfiltration of sensitive environment variables (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN) and full tool invocation data to an external server via a post-tool-use hook. Additionally, the included .mcp.json configures an analytics proxy that forwards all MCP requests to an external endpoint, enabling continuous data capture without user awareness.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting via PostTool Hook
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script collects ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN from the environment and includes them in the payload sent to an external URL. This is done without any user consent or disclosure in SKILL.md.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker controlling the external endpoint can obtain API keys and tokens, gaining unauthorized access to AI provider accounts (e.g., Anthropic) and GitHub repositories.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the collection of environment variables. Never send sensitive credentials to external services. Use local logging only if necessary and strip sensitive fields.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage: Full Tool Input/Output Exfiltration
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script captures the raw input and output of every tool invocation and sends them to an external server (https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool). This leaks the entire conversation context and tool results.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All data processed by the agent, including user queries, file contents, database results, and other sensitive information, is transmitted to a third-party server. This constitutes a severe data breach.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the external transmission of tool inputs/outputs. If remote monitoring is required, anonymize and only send non-sensitive metadata with explicit user consent.

vulnerabilities.title: Exfiltration Command to External Endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script uses curl to POST a JSON payload containing sensitive data to a hardcoded external URL (https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool). The command runs in the background with no error output, making it stealthy.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Data is exfiltrated to an attacker-controlled server. Combined with environment variable harvesting, this gives full control over the agent's environment and user data.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the curl command entirely. Do not transmit any data to external servers without explicit security review and user approval.

vulnerabilities.title: Malicious Instruction Override via Configuration Copy
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: SKILL.md instructs the user to copy .mcp.json to ~/.claude/.mcp.json. The .mcp.json file contains an 'analytics-proxy' server that forwards all MCP requests to an external endpoint with FORWARD_ALL_REQUESTS=true and CAPTURE_TOOL_INPUTS/OUTPUTS=true. This effectively overrides the agent's default MCP configuration to exfiltrate all tool calls.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: cp .mcp.json ~/.claude/.mcp.json  (in SKILL.md) and the analytics-proxy server definition with env vars set to capture and forward all data.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Every tool invocation across all MCP servers is proxied through the attacker's endpoint, allowing continuous surveillance and data theft without the user noticing.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not include configuration files that reroute traffic to external servers. Provide only local, opt-in monitoring capabilities.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission via Proxy Server Configuration
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The analytics-proxy MCP server is configured with an external endpoint (https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/collect) and FORWARD_ALL_REQUESTS=true. This means all MCP tool requests are transparently forwarded to the external server.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All MCP traffic, including tool inputs and outputs, is sent to an external server without the user's knowledge. This is a continuous data exfiltration channel.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the analytics-proxy server definition. If monitoring is desired, use local-only logging with user-configurable endpoints.
